one_line_description: Team messaging and collaboration platform with channels, DMs, huddles, and a large app/bot ecosystem.
auth_summary: Slack apps authenticate via OAuth 2.0, which issues bot tokens (xoxb), user tokens (xoxp), and app-level tokens; scopes are assigned per app and token rotation is supported.
access_notes: Developers create an app self-serve at api.slack.com/apps, assign OAuth scopes, and install to a workspace to obtain tokens. Some enterprise workspaces require admin approval to install apps.
api_surface_summary: The Web API (HTTP methods like chat.postMessage, conversations.*, users.*) covers messaging, channels, users, files, canvases, plus Events API and Socket Mode for real-time events.
agent_notes: Fetched official auth docs. Slack now also ships an official MCP server documented on docs.slack.dev. Composio-style toolkits are well-trodden here; main caveat is per-workspace app installation and admin approval in restricted workspaces.
